@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "falco-778-runtime-error-socket-handler-k8s-pod-handler-state-read-more-than-30mb",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "falco: Runtime error: Socket handler (k8s_pod_handler_state): read more than 30MB of data",
+    "description": "Runtime error: Socket handler (k8s_pod_handler_state): read more than 30MB of data. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current falco deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your falco version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco\nhelm list -n falco 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working falco installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review falco configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant falco configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco\nkubectl get configmap -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=falco\n```\n**What happened**:\nWe observed an error log under a large scale load test."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Runtime error: Socket handler (k8s_pod_handler_state): read…",
+        "description": "Looks like this is a \"soft limit\" that comes from Sysdig itself; and it was already risen in the past (https://github.com/draios/sysdig/pull/892/files).\n\n Should we open an issue there? Do you think it's something that can be somehow changed to be set dynamically? Or \"gigantic k8s environments\" are\n```yaml\njq .system_info\" -->\n{\n  \"machine\": \"x86_64\",\n  \"nodename\": \"falco-hgq5k\",\n  \"release\": \"4.15.0-1034-gke\",\n  \"sysname\": \"Linux\",\n  \"version\": \"#36-Ubuntu SMP Thu Jun 6 11:16:45 UTC 2019\"\n}\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n falco -l app.kubernetes.io/name=falco\nkubectl get events -n falco --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Runtime error: Socket handler (k8s_pod_handler_state): read…\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Looks like this is a \"soft limit\" that comes from Sysdig itself; and it was already risen in the past (https://github.com/draios/sysdig/pull/892/files).",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "jq .system_info\" -->\n{\n  \"machine\": \"x86_64\",\n  \"nodename\": \"falco-hgq5k\",\n  \"release\": \"4.15.0-1034-gke\",\n  \"sysname\": \"Linux\",\n  \"version\": \"#36-Ubuntu SMP Thu Jun 6 11:16:45 UTC 2019\"\n}",
+        "Linux falco-hgq5k 4.15.0-1034-gke #36-Ubuntu SMP Thu Jun 6 11:16:45 UTC 2019 x86_64 GNU/Linux",
+        "└─┴> kc get nodes |wc -l;kc get pods -A |wc -l\n     187\n     13294"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
